edge_hypothesis: The 'rubber band' snap-back is the highest-velocity move pattern in crypto. When price pulls back deeply within an established uptrend, the recovery rally tends to be explosive — driven by cascading liquidations of late shorts, dip-buying algorithms, and fear-of-missing-out buying. The existing rsi_pullback specs (rp4f2w8n, rp8f3m7v) test fast EMAs (100, 50) with RSI ≤ 40-45. But RSI ≤ 40 is a MILD pullback — it fires frequently and includes shallow retracements that lack snap-back velocity. The untested frontier is ULTRA-DEEP pullbacks: RSI ≤ 30 within an uptrend (or RSI ≥ 70 within a downtrend). RSI ≤ 30 is rare — maybe 5-15 signals per 1000 bars on 1h — but when it occurs in an ESTABLISHED uptrend (close > EMA_100), it represents a severe dislocation from the dominant trend. These are panic selloffs within bull markets, flash crashes, or aggressive profit-taking that temporarily pushes RSI to extreme levels while the broader trend structure (EMA_100 slope) remains intact. The snap-back from RSI ≤ 30 in an uptrend is typically violent: price recovers 60-80% of the pullback within 3-6 bars as sellers exhaust and trend-followers re-enter. With 8:1 R:R and a time cap, we capture only the explosive snap-backs while killing the few cases where RSI ≤ 30 is the START of a trend reversal (these trades will slowly bleed and get killed by the time cap before hitting the full stop). Using EMA_100 (17 days on 1h) instead of EMA_200 (33 days) catches trends earlier — critical in crypto where trends are shorter-lived. EMA_9 slope confirmation ensures the immediate momentum has started turning up before entry. Invalidation: if PF < 0.8, ultra-deep pullbacks in uptrends are more often trend reversals than snap-backs — the 'rubber band' thesis fails for crypto. If trade count < 20, the signal is too rare to be practically useful even if PF is high.

variants.name: rsi_pullback_1h_deep30_ema100_8to1
variants.description: Ultra-deep pullback entry on ETH/1h. Close > EMA_100 (established uptrend), EMA_9 slope rising (momentum turning), RSI_14 ≤ 30 (severe oversold dislocation). 8:1 R:R with 2.0x ATR stop, 16.0x ATR TP, and 6-bar time cap (6 hours). This is the core 'rubber band' test. RSI ≤ 30 in an uptrend is rare (~5-15 signals per 1000 bars) but should produce the highest snap-back velocity. The 6-bar time cap is aggressive — if the snap-back doesn't start within 6 hours, the pullback is likely a trend reversal and should be cut. Expected: 20-50 trades over backtest period, high PF (> 1.5) if hypothesis holds, very low DD because losses are cut quickly by time cap.
entry_long: Close > EMA_100 (price above medium-term trend — established uptrend).
EMA_9 slope rising (EMA_9 current bar > EMA_9 previous bar — immediate momentum turning up).
RSI_14 ≤ 30 (ultra-deep oversold — severe pullback within uptrend).
entry_short: Close < EMA_100 (price below medium-term trend — established downtrend).
EMA_9 slope falling (EMA_9 current bar < EMA_9 previous bar — immediate momentum turning down).
RSI_14 ≥ 70 (ultra-deep overbought — severe rally within downtrend).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (16.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Exit at market after 6 bars if neither TP nor SL has been hit (time-decay exit — snap-backs are fast or they're reversals).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < 0.8, lower RSI threshold to 25 or raise to 35 — calibrate the 'deep enough' threshold
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 16.0x ATR_14 from entry.
Time exit after 6 bars at market price.

components.notes: EMA_100 trend filter + EMA_9 slope — fast trend detection with immediate momentum confirmation
RSI_14 ≤ 30 — ultra-deep pullback, rubber band snap-back entry
ATR_14 for 2.0x stop / 16.0x TP — 8:1 R:R with 6-bar time cap

variants.name: rsi_pullback_1h_deep35_ema50_7to1
variants.description: Moderately deep pullback on ETH/1h with ultra-fast trend detection. Close > EMA_50 (8 days — very fast trend, catches trends early), EMA_9 slope rising, RSI_14 ≤ 35 (deep but not extreme). 7:1 R:R with 8-bar time cap. This variant tests two differences from the core: (1) EMA_50 vs EMA_100 — does catching trends earlier produce more signals and better PF? (2) RSI ≤ 35 vs ≤ 30 — is the slightly less extreme pullback threshold better because it fires more often while still capturing high-velocity snap-backs? The 8-bar time cap gives 33% more room than the 6-bar variant. At 7:1 R:R, direct comparison to MACD 7:1 (PF=1.712) reveals whether deep-pullback RSI can match MACD momentum's signal quality.
entry_long: Close > EMA_50 (price above short-term trend — fast trend detection).
EMA_9 slope rising (immediate momentum turning up).
RSI_14 ≤ 35 (deep oversold — significant pullback within uptrend).
entry_short: EMA_9 slope falling (immediate momentum turning down).
RSI_14 ≥ 65 (deep overbought — significant rally within downtrend).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (14.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Exit at market after 8 bars if neither TP nor SL has been hit (time-decay exit).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF at EMA_50 < PF at EMA_100, faster trend detection catches MORE noise — EMA_100 is the minimum viable trend filter for pullbacks
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 14.0x ATR_14 from entry.
Time exit after 8 bars at market price.

components.notes: EMA_50 ultra-fast trend filter + EMA_9 slope — catches trends within 8 days
RSI_14 ≤ 35 — deep pullback, snap-back entry with moderate selectivity
ATR_14 for 2.0x stop / 14.0x TP — 7:1 R:R with 8-bar time cap

variants.name: rsi_pullback_4h_deep30_ema100_9to1
variants.description: Ultra-deep pullback on ETH/4h with EMA_100 trend filter (17 days), EMA_21 slope confirmation, RSI_14 ≤ 30. 9:1 R:R (2.0x stop, 18.0x TP) with 5-bar time cap (20 hours). The 4h timeframe tests whether deep pullback signals are CLEANER on higher timeframes. On 4h, RSI ≤ 30 is extremely rare (maybe 3-10 signals over the backtest period) but each signal represents a multi-day panic event within an established trend. These are the highest-conviction snap-back setups because on 4h, the trend structure (EMA_100) is well-established and unlikely to reverse from a single panic event. The 5-bar (20 hour) time cap demands the recovery start within one trading day. At 9:1 R:R, this captures only the most explosive multi-day recoveries.
entry_long: Close > EMA_100 (price above medium-term trend — established multi-week uptrend on 4h).
EMA_21 slope rising (EMA_21 current bar > EMA_21 previous bar — intermediate momentum turning).
RSI_14 ≤ 30 (ultra-deep oversold — rare panic event within established uptrend).
entry_short: Close < EMA_100 (price below medium-term trend — established downtrend).
EMA_21 slope falling (intermediate momentum turning down).
RSI_14 ≥ 70 (ultra-deep overbought — rare euphoria event within established downtrend).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (18.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Exit at market after 5 bars if neither TP nor SL has been hit (time-decay exit — recovery must start within one trading day on 4h).
Hypothesis failure mode: if trade count < 10, signal too rare for statistical validity — consider RSI ≤ 35 threshold. If PF > 2.0, this is the highest-edge entry in the system.
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 18.0x ATR_14 from entry.
Time exit after 5 bars at market price.

components.notes: EMA_100 trend filter + EMA_21 slope — established trend with intermediate momentum confirmation on 4h
RSI_14 ≤ 30 — ultra-deep pullback, rare panic event in established trend
ATR_14 for 2.0x stop / 18.0x TP — 9:1 R:R with 5-bar time cap (20 hours on 4h)
